channel.title: Comments for page "Project Proposal 1964-238: "The Eater of Worlds""
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Project Proposal 1964-238: "The Eater of Worlds""

item.content:encoded:
An absolutely brilliant piece, that manages to tackle a somewhat overused subject in an imaginative and interesting way (the bit about the brain matter was also particularly fun).

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, insofar as they're both alternate translations of The Bhagavad Gita's line about time famously quoted by Oppenheimer: "I am become death, the destroyer of worlds", yes, they are related.

Galactus from the Marvel universe is also known as The Eater of Worlds for similar reasons.

(I've noticed you do this a lot…are you familiar with the concept of Fan Myopia? Not making fun, just an observation.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is page is nicknamed "eater of worlds".

There is a boss in terraria called the eater of worlds, its just a giant worm.

Coincidence?

I

THINK

NOT

item.title: Re: holy SHIT
item.content:encoded: That's at least two things in one address, the Factory GOI and art gallery. Impressive

item.title: Re: holy SHIT
item.content:encoded: HOLY SHIT INDEED

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't you just hate when you need live grey matter samples for your sentient atomic bomb sculpture? Especially when it takes over a year—it really disrupts the creative flow.

Casual dark humor aside (or perhaps not, given the page's subject material) this was really well done. The way the artist talks about his creative process almost makes it all seem downright normal, and that's what sold it for me. Upvoted.

item.title: Re: holy SHIT
item.content:encoded: I was wondering when someone was gonna notice that. :)

item.title: Re: holy SHIT
item.content:encoded: Ha. Isn't it great when an author adds little details like that?

item.title: holy SHIT
item.content:encoded:
Getting a nuclear bomb was the easy part - a couple phone calls, a quick stop by 231 East 47th Street, and they hauled one up from the basement

I thought, hm, that looks like a real address. I wonder if we should really use it - if this was being presented as a Foundation document, it'd be redacted, but AWCY proposals don't redact shit… hm, I wonder what's at that address.

Holy shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to say, I'm really digging the new AWCY format. I thought this article was pretty good at first, but the intent part sold me. Well done overall, upvoted wholeheartedly!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this backstory really adds to the original article. I especially liked the line about the solenoids. Not sure why, it just seemed sort of funny. I kind of wish the Intent section were a little less narrative and a little more personal explanation, but that's a relatively minor thing for me. The stuff all around it makes up for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this, I find interesting both the format (specially the author's insight bit) and the content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spectacular. I look forward to more of these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From the rules:

The contest requires that at least two or three objects be written from the perspective of the GoI you were assigned. Note: This does NOT mean to take the SCP Format and try to put a fun, neato little twist on it. The SCP Format is used by the Foundation. Therefore, it was written for use in the Foundation.

You will be expected to consider the GoI carefully, consider their goals and motivations, and come up with a new, creative format. For example, Are We Cool Yet? might write a poem to classify things. The Factory might have production and product testing notes. Marshall, Carter, and Dark might have an auction list with nice, long write-ups. Get creative.

As with the tales, the Objects have certain requirements as well:

At least one object must be a re-imagining of a Foundation object.
At least one object must be entirely new.

Thus they were REQUIRED to re-imagine at least one of the "mainlisters", from AWCY's perspective. But they are also required to do at least one that is brand new.

So don't worry, you're going to see that from every team if they follow the rules correctly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't the word "functional" be struck through?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This story was fascinating and filled me with a sense of deep unease (that's a compliment). I liked how this person could just BUY an atomic bomb and snippings of people's brains; that seems to imply some sort of far-reaching occult/artistic black market underground, which is pretty intriguing! The internal debate about the artistic integrity of a defused bomb made me feel like I should be laughing nervously. Great work!

(After web searching for 10 minutes or so, I wasn't able to find whether the name of MIT's introductory nuclear energy course was 'Intro to Atomic Energy'. If you have a better source, I'd be interested to hear about it! If you don't, though, I'm sure it's as good a name as any. ;) Fascinating stuff though… http://libraries.mit.edu/mithistory/research/schools-and-departments/school-of-engineering/department-of-nuclear-science-and-engineering/ )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank God for this contest. Only two entries have been submitted and I've already revolutionized my view on a GoI I once believed was boring and overused. I can only hope that future pieces will do the same for their own respective GoIs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic, and I really like AWCY's format, but I hope that all of the objects won't just be redone mainlisters from the GoI's perspective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Visitors are to be encouraged to speak to and question the bomb, thus allowing the bomb's responses to reflect its opinions as a weapon on the growing danger of global nuclear war, and allowing the visitor to be confronted with the reality of global annihilation and to challenge their own preconceptions about the matter.

This had me freaked out and engrossed at the same time. This is a really weird but fascinating article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha. Very cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This a great reimagining and a nice blend of narrative and description. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is our first object article for the AWCY canon. This is a reimagining of the original SCP-1286, "Sad Man", by Faminepulse.
